title: Lupinaster amabilis
taxonomy: Lupinaster amabilis belongs to the family Oleaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the olive family. This family includes over 600 species of plants, many of which are native to the Northern Hemisphere. Lupinaster amabilis is a member of the genus Lupinaster, which consists of small to medium-sized shrubs or trees. The genus is native to the Mediterranean region and is characterized by its fragrant flowers and small, elliptical leaves. As a member of the Oleaceae family, Lupinaster amabilis shares characteristics with other plants in the same family, such as olive trees and jasmine.
